page: Vultr vcg-mi355x-252c-2872g-2304vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server built on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with an AMD processor providing 252 vCPUs and a system memory capacity of 2872.0 GB. This memory configuration yields approximately 11.4 GB of RAM per core, supporting large-scale in-memory data structures and parallel processing tasks.

The server features a highly dense graphics acceleration subsystem consisting of eight AMD MI355X GPUs from the CDNA4 family, delivering a combined total of 2304 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided by 14336 GB of NVMe SSDs, which facilitates high-throughput data access. Additionally, the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

This hardware profile is engineered for resource-intensive workloads. The combination of massive GPU memory, high vCPU count, and extensive local NVMe storage makes this server suitable for large-scale artificial intelligence training, large language model fine-tuning, and complex scientific simulations.

This high-density hardware profile is optimized for large-scale artificial intelligence training, large language model fine-tuning, and high-performance computing tasks that require substantial GPU memory and compute resources.
